title: Supply Chain Career Explorer
subtitle: Answer a few quick questions to discover which supply chain path might fit you best.
intro: There is no single “right” career in supply chain. This short quiz highlights roles where your interests and strengths may align—from planning and analytics to logistics and procurement. Use it as a conversation starter, not a final decision.

procurement.title: Procurement & Strategic Sourcing
procurement.tagline: You build value through relationships and smart buying decisions.
procurement.description: Procurement professionals negotiate with suppliers, manage contracts, and ensure the right materials and services arrive at the right cost and quality. You thrive when balancing analytics with relationship-building.

logistics.title: Logistics & Transportation
logistics.tagline: You keep products moving efficiently across the network.
logistics.description: Logistics focuses on how goods flow from origin to customer—modes of transport, routing, carrier management, and delivery performance. If you like solving puzzles under time pressure, this path offers constant action.

warehousing.title: Warehousing & Distribution
warehousing.tagline: You optimize what happens inside the four walls.
warehousing.description: Distribution centers are the heartbeat of fulfillment. Roles here cover layout, inventory accuracy, picking/packing, automation, and safety—turning storage into speed and reliability.

planning.title: Demand Planning & S&OP
planning.tagline: You connect forecasts, sales, and operations into one plan.
planning.description: Planners predict what customers will need and align production, inventory, and sales. Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) brings cross-functional teams together to balance service, cost, and capacity.

analytics.title: Supply Chain Analytics
analytics.tagline: You turn data into decisions the business can act on.
analytics.description: Analytics roles use data to improve visibility, predict disruptions, and measure performance. You might build dashboards, run models, or dig into KPIs that leaders use every week.

operations.title: Operations & Production
operations.tagline: You improve how products are made and delivered at scale.
operations.description: Operations managers oversee plants, production lines, or end-to-end processes—quality, throughput, continuous improvement, and people leadership on the floor.

sustainability.title: Sustainability & Responsible Supply Chain
sustainability.tagline: You help organizations do well by doing good.
sustainability.description: This growing field addresses carbon footprint, ethical sourcing, circular economy, and ESG reporting. You connect environmental and social goals with everyday supply chain choices.

consulting.title: Consulting & Project Leadership
consulting.tagline: You love variety, client impact, and solving new problems.
consulting.description: Consultants and project leads work across industries—designing network strategy, implementing systems, or leading transformation. Ideal if you enjoy learning fast and presenting to executives.

questions.text: What kind of work sounds most exciting to you?

answers.text: Negotiating deals and building supplier partnerships
Coordinating trucks, ships, or routes to hit delivery goals
Improving how a warehouse or fulfillment center runs
Forecasting demand and balancing supply with sales

questions.text: Which class project would you enjoy most?

answers.text: Building a dashboard or model from a messy dataset
Mapping a greener sourcing strategy for a product line
Redesigning a factory or line to reduce waste and delays
Pitching a network redesign to a leadership team

questions.text: What is your strongest skill right now?

answers.text: Numbers, spreadsheets, and spotting patterns
Communication and influencing others
Organizing people and processes on the ground
Staying calm when plans change at the last minute

questions.text: What impact do you want to have in your career?

answers.text: Reduce cost and risk while keeping partners strong
Get the right product to the right place on time
Help the company plan ahead with fewer surprises
Make supply chains more ethical and sustainable

questions.text: Which work environment appeals to you?

answers.text: Mix of office and site visits with different clients
Fast-paced operations floor or distribution center
Collaborative planning meetings with sales and ops
Quiet focus time with data and tools

questions.text: Pick the headline you would be proudest to own:

answers.text: “Cut transportation spend 12% without hurting service”
“Launched ethical sourcing standards across our top suppliers”
“Improved forecast accuracy and freed $2M in inventory”
“Led a cross-site productivity program that scaled nationally”
